seed_problem: Themed grid network; remove at most k links to maximize the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores achieved delay.
mutation: output: keep the interdiction goal but relax the network from a pure grid to a grid-plus-shortcut multigraph with heavy-tailed edge costs, so the optimal budget-k shortest-path interdiction is NP-hard and greedy/local-search strategies diverge.
title: Sabotaged Sourdough: Flour-Route Interdiction
strategies: do-nothing baseline: close no links (achieves the original route length)
greedy weight heuristic: repeatedly close the heaviest link on the current shortest route while keeping mill-bakery connectivity
best-improvement local search: at each step evaluate the true post-closure shortest route for every candidate link and commit the best
randomized multi-restart: perturb the greedy-best choices across restarts and keep the interdiction set that maximizes final delay
